title: Objects, Packfiles, Reachability, and Integrity · Collaboration Transfer
summary: An independent maintainer receives a different graph, repository policy, and failure constraint and must transfer objects, packfiles, reachability, and integrity into a new object-database integrity and reachability report. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of object-database integrity and reachability report with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Trace blobs, trees, commits, annotated tags, parent edges, and reference roots as an immutable reachable object graph.
Explain loose objects, pack indexes, delta bases, multi-pack indexes, and why storage representation does not change object identity.
Distinguish reachable, unreachable, dangling, promised, missing, and corrupt objects using fsck and reference evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-advanced cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model
steps.instruction:
lab case — Case G23 is a lab in a disposable repository. the reported tree is unreachable but still present rather than corrupt or promised. The learner must predict fsck classifies connectivity and names candidate unreachable objects without pruning them, run git fsck --full --no-reflogs --unreachable, explain the candidate tree is present and reachable from the retained incident commit only, test this changed condition: remove the retained incident root in the simulator and observe the commit become unreachable, and preserve this recovery boundary: create refs/recovery/integrity before any repack expiration or prune operation.

What must be established before implementation?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for trace blobs, trees, commits, annotated tags, parent edges, and reference roots as an immutable reachable object graph.

steps.title: Extend the cumulative artifact
steps.instruction: Append an Advanced Git evidence block headed "# Evidence: gita-integrity-preservation-l1w0kb" with exact before and after refs; a falsifiable prediction; the bounded simulator command; expected, observed, tree, behavior, changed-case, and recovery evidence; and one honest real-repository transfer limit.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Gita Integrity Preservation into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: gita-integrity-preservation-l1w0kb
# scenario-key: trace-l1w0kb probe-bk0w1l boundary-w0kbl1 owner-l1w0kbx fault-l1w0kbz
# before-ref: main=a11cel1 candidate=b00b1kb
# hypothesis: the reported tree is unreachable but still present rather than corrupt or promised
git fsck --full --no-reflogs --unreachable
# expected: fsck classifies connectivity and names candidate unreachable objects without pruning them
# observation: the candidate tree is present and reachable from the retained incident commit only
# changed-case: remove the retained incident root in the simulator and observe the commit become unreachable
# after-ref: main=a11cel1 candidate=c0ffeeb
# tree-proof: the affected tree and path set match the predicted repository transition
# behavior-proof: a changed input preserves the named product or release invariant
# recovery: create refs/recovery/integrity before any repack expiration or prune operation
# transfer-limit: quarantine and copy real object storage before repair because this simulator has no pack corruption
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.
